concepts.label: Resistor
concepts.definition: Passive two-terminal component that opposes electric current flow, producing a voltage drop proportional to current (Ohm's law)

concepts.label: Carbon Film Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor made by depositing a thin carbon film on a ceramic substrate, offering 5% tolerance typically

concepts.label: Metal Film Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor using a thin metal film on ceramic, offering 1% or better tolerance and low noise

concepts.label: Metal Oxide Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor using metal oxide film, offering high temperature stability and surge handling

concepts.label: Wire-Wound Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor made by winding resistance wire around a core, used for high-power applications

concepts.label: Thick Film Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor made by screen-printing resistive paste onto a substrate, common in SMD packages

concepts.label: Thin Film Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor made by sputtering a thin resistive layer, offering very tight tolerance (0.1%) and low TCR

concepts.label: Potentiometer
concepts.definition: Three-terminal variable resistor with an adjustable wiper contact for voltage division

concepts.label: Trimmer Potentiometer
concepts.definition: Small adjustable potentiometer for fine-tuning circuits, typically set once during calibration

concepts.label: Rheostat
concepts.definition: Two-terminal variable resistor used to control current flow in a circuit

concepts.label: Thermistor
concepts.definition: Temperature-sensitive resistor whose resistance changes significantly with temperature

concepts.label: NTC Thermistor
concepts.definition: Negative temperature coefficient thermistor whose resistance decreases as temperature rises

concepts.label: PTC Thermistor
concepts.definition: Positive temperature coefficient thermistor whose resistance increases as temperature rises

concepts.label: Varistor
concepts.definition: Voltage-dependent resistor (VDR) that changes resistance with applied voltage, used for surge protection

concepts.label: Metal Oxide Varistor
concepts.definition: MOV type varistor using zinc oxide, widely used for transient voltage suppression

concepts.label: Photoresistor
concepts.definition: Light-dependent resistor (LDR) whose resistance decreases with increasing light intensity

concepts.label: Shunt Resistor
concepts.definition: Low-value precision resistor used to measure current by sensing voltage drop across it

concepts.label: Ballast Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor used to limit current in a circuit, commonly in LED and fluorescent lamp drivers

concepts.label: Pull-Up Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor connected between a signal line and positive supply to ensure a defined high logic level

concepts.label: Pull-Down Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor connected between a signal line and ground to ensure a defined low logic level

concepts.label: Fusible Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor designed to act as a fuse by open-circuiting when power rating is exceeded

concepts.label: Zero-Ohm Resistor
concepts.definition: Resistor with near-zero resistance used as a jumper wire in PCB manufacturing

concepts.label: Resistor Network
concepts.definition: Multiple resistors packaged in a single component, used for bus termination and pull-up arrays

concepts.label: Capacitor
concepts.definition: Passive two-terminal component that stores electrical energy in an electric field between two conductive plates

concepts.label: Ceramic Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor using ceramic dielectric, available as MLCC (multilayer) for compact high-frequency applications

concepts.label: MLCC
concepts.definition: Multilayer Ceramic Capacitor with alternating ceramic and metal layers for high capacitance in small packages

concepts.label: Electrolytic Capacitor
concepts.definition: Polarized capacitor using an electrolyte as cathode, providing high capacitance values for filtering

concepts.label: Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitor
concepts.definition: Electrolytic capacitor using aluminum oxide dielectric, common for power supply filtering

concepts.label: Tantalum Capacitor
concepts.definition: Electrolytic capacitor using tantalum pentoxide dielectric, offering stable capacitance in small size

concepts.label: Niobium Capacitor
concepts.definition: Electrolytic capacitor using niobium oxide dielectric as an alternative to tantalum

concepts.label: Polymer Capacitor
concepts.definition: Electrolytic capacitor using conductive polymer as electrolyte, offering low ESR and long life

concepts.label: Film Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor using thin plastic film as dielectric, offering tight tolerance and self-healing properties

concepts.label: Polyester Film Capacitor
concepts.definition: Film capacitor using polyester (Mylar) dielectric, general-purpose with moderate performance

concepts.label: Polypropylene Capacitor
concepts.definition: Film capacitor using polypropylene dielectric, preferred for audio and high-frequency applications

concepts.label: Polystyrene Capacitor
concepts.definition: Film capacitor using polystyrene dielectric, offering very low dielectric absorption

concepts.label: Mica Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor using mica mineral as dielectric, offering high stability and low loss at RF frequencies

concepts.label: Silver Mica Capacitor
concepts.definition: Mica capacitor with silver electrodes, used in precision RF and oscillator circuits

concepts.label: Glass Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor using glass dielectric for ultra-stable performance in military/aerospace applications

concepts.label: Supercapacitor
concepts.definition: Electrochemical capacitor (EDLC) storing energy via double-layer capacitance, bridging gap between capacitors and batteries

concepts.label: Trimmer Capacitor
concepts.definition: Small variable capacitor for fine-tuning resonant circuits, adjusted with a screwdriver

concepts.label: Variable Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor whose capacitance can be mechanically adjusted, historically used in radio tuning

concepts.label: Vacuum Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor using vacuum as dielectric for very high voltage and RF power applications

concepts.label: Feed-Through Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor designed for EMI filtering at panel or PCB boundaries

concepts.label: Safety Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor rated for line-to-line (X class) or line-to-ground (Y class) AC mains applications

concepts.label: Inductor
concepts.definition: Passive component that stores energy in a magnetic field when current flows through a coil of wire

concepts.label: Air Core Inductor
concepts.definition: Inductor without a magnetic core, offering linear behavior and use at very high frequencies

concepts.label: Ferrite Core Inductor
concepts.definition: Inductor using ferrite magnetic core for higher inductance in compact size

concepts.label: Iron Core Inductor
concepts.definition: Inductor using laminated iron core for high inductance at power frequencies

concepts.label: Toroidal Inductor
concepts.definition: Inductor wound on a toroidal (doughnut-shaped) core for low electromagnetic interference

concepts.label: Choke
concepts.definition: Inductor designed to block high-frequency AC while passing DC, used in power supply filtering

concepts.label: Common Mode Choke
concepts.definition: Dual-winding inductor that suppresses common-mode noise while passing differential signals

concepts.label: RF Choke
concepts.definition: Inductor optimized to block radio frequency signals from passing through a conductor

concepts.label: Power Inductor
concepts.definition: Inductor designed for DC-DC converter applications, handling high current with low DCR

concepts.label: Coupled Inductor
concepts.definition: Two or more inductors sharing a magnetic core, used in multiphase power supplies

concepts.label: Ferrite Bead
concepts.definition: Passive component that suppresses high-frequency noise by converting it to heat in ferrite material

concepts.label: Transformer
concepts.definition: Device using electromagnetic induction between coupled coils to transfer energy and change voltage levels

concepts.label: Step-Up Transformer
concepts.definition: Transformer with more secondary than primary turns, increasing voltage while decreasing current

concepts.label: Step-Down Transformer
concepts.definition: Transformer with fewer secondary than primary turns, decreasing voltage while increasing current

concepts.label: Isolation Transformer
concepts.definition: Transformer with 1:1 turns ratio providing galvanic isolation between circuits

concepts.label: Autotransformer
concepts.definition: Transformer with a single tapped winding serving as both primary and secondary

concepts.label: Current Transformer
concepts.definition: Transformer designed to produce a reduced current proportional to the current in its primary

concepts.label: Pulse Transformer
concepts.definition: Transformer optimized for transmitting rectangular pulses with minimal distortion

concepts.label: Flyback Transformer
concepts.definition: Coupled inductor used in flyback converter topologies for isolated power supplies

concepts.label: Planar Transformer
concepts.definition: Transformer using flat PCB windings instead of wire, for compact high-frequency power conversion

concepts.label: Diode
concepts.definition: Semiconductor device allowing current flow in one direction, with a p-n junction

concepts.label: Rectifier Diode
concepts.definition: Standard p-n junction diode designed for converting AC to DC in power supplies

concepts.label: Schottky Diode
concepts.definition: Metal-semiconductor junction diode with low forward voltage drop (~0.3V) and fast switching

concepts.label: Zener Diode
concepts.definition: Diode designed to operate in reverse breakdown at a specific voltage, used for voltage regulation

concepts.label: TVS Diode
concepts.definition: Transient voltage suppressor diode that clamps voltage spikes to protect sensitive circuits

concepts.label: Varactor Diode
concepts.definition: Diode whose junction capacitance varies with reverse voltage, used in voltage-controlled oscillators

concepts.label: PIN Diode
concepts.definition: Diode with an intrinsic region between p and n layers, used as RF switch and attenuator

concepts.label: Tunnel Diode
concepts.definition: Heavily doped p-n junction exhibiting negative differential resistance via quantum tunneling

concepts.label: Gunn Diode
concepts.definition: Transferred-electron device used to generate microwave oscillations

concepts.label: IMPATT Diode
concepts.definition: Impact ionization avalanche transit-time diode for generating microwave power

concepts.label: Step Recovery Diode
concepts.definition: Diode that stores charge and releases it abruptly, generating sharp pulses for frequency multiplication

concepts.label: Laser Diode
concepts.definition: Semiconductor diode that emits coherent light through stimulated emission

concepts.label: Photodiode
concepts.definition: Semiconductor diode that converts light into electrical current via the photovoltaic effect

concepts.label: Avalanche Photodiode
concepts.definition: Photodiode operating in reverse bias near breakdown for internal gain via impact ionization

concepts.label: LED
concepts.definition: Light-emitting diode that produces light when forward-biased current causes electron-hole recombination

concepts.label: RGB LED
concepts.definition: LED package containing red, green, and blue emitters for color mixing

concepts.label: SMD LED
concepts.definition: Surface-mount LED in standardized packages (0402, 0603, 0805) for PCB assembly

concepts.label: High-Power LED
concepts.definition: LED designed to operate at 1W or more, requiring thermal management

concepts.label: UV LED
concepts.definition: Light-emitting diode producing ultraviolet radiation, used in curing and sterilization

concepts.label: Infrared LED
concepts.definition: LED emitting infrared light, used in remote controls and proximity sensors

concepts.label: OLED Pixel
concepts.definition: Organic LED element that emits light from organic compounds, used in display pixels

concepts.label: Addressable LED
concepts.definition: LED with integrated driver IC (e.g., WS2812B/NeoPixel) allowing individual control via serial data

concepts.label: Transistor
concepts.definition: Semiconductor device used to amplify or switch electronic signals, the building block of modern electronics

concepts.label: BJT
concepts.definition: Bipolar junction transistor using both electron and hole carriers, available as NPN or PNP

concepts.label: NPN Transistor
concepts.definition: BJT where current flows from collector to emitter when base-emitter junction is forward-biased

concepts.label: PNP Transistor
concepts.definition: BJT where current flows from emitter to collector when base is pulled below emitter voltage

concepts.label: Darlington Transistor
concepts.definition: Compound transistor pairing two BJTs for very high current gain (beta squared)

concepts.label: Phototransistor
concepts.definition: Transistor whose base current is generated by incident light, combining photodetection with gain

concepts.label: MOSFET
concepts.definition: Metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistor controlled by gate voltage, dominant in digital and power electronics

concepts.label: N-Channel MOSFET
concepts.definition: MOSFET that conducts when positive gate-source voltage exceeds threshold, majority carriers are electrons

concepts.label: P-Channel MOSFET
concepts.definition: MOSFET that conducts when gate voltage is pulled below source by threshold voltage

concepts.label: Enhancement-Mode MOSFET
concepts.definition: MOSFET that is normally off and requires gate voltage to create a conducting channel

concepts.label: Depletion-Mode MOSFET
concepts.definition: MOSFET that is normally on and requires gate voltage to pinch off the channel

concepts.label: Power MOSFET
concepts.definition: MOSFET designed for high-current switching with low on-resistance (RDS_on)

concepts.label: JFET
concepts.definition: Junction field-effect transistor using a reverse-biased p-n junction as gate, normally-on device

concepts.label: IGBT
concepts.definition: Insulated-gate bipolar transistor combining MOSFET gate with BJT output for high-voltage high-current switching

concepts.label: HEMT
concepts.definition: High electron mobility transistor using heterojunction for very high frequency operation (GaN, GaAs)

concepts.label: FinFET
concepts.definition: Multi-gate transistor with fin-shaped channel providing better electrostatic control at nanometer nodes

concepts.label: GAA FET
concepts.definition: Gate-all-around FET with channel surrounded by gate on all sides, successor to FinFET at sub-3nm

concepts.label: Unijunction Transistor
concepts.definition: Three-terminal device with single junction used as relaxation oscillator trigger

concepts.label: SiC MOSFET
concepts.definition: Silicon carbide MOSFET offering high voltage, high temperature, and low switching loss for power electronics

concepts.label: GaN HEMT
concepts.definition: Gallium nitride high-electron-mobility transistor for high-frequency high-efficiency power conversion

concepts.label: Thyristor
concepts.definition: Four-layer PNPN semiconductor device that latches on when triggered, used in power control

concepts.label: SCR
concepts.definition: Silicon-controlled rectifier, a thyristor triggered by gate current, used for AC power control

concepts.label: TRIAC
concepts.definition: Bidirectional thyristor that can conduct in both directions, used for AC dimming and motor control

concepts.label: DIAC
concepts.definition: Bidirectional trigger diode that conducts when voltage exceeds breakover threshold in either direction

concepts.label: GTO
concepts.definition: Gate turn-off thyristor that can be turned off by negative gate current

concepts.label: Op-Amp
concepts.definition: Operational amplifier: high-gain differential voltage amplifier with inverting and non-inverting inputs

concepts.label: Instrumentation Amplifier
concepts.definition: Precision differential amplifier with high CMRR and adjustable gain, used in sensor signal conditioning

concepts.label: Comparator
concepts.definition: IC that compares two voltages and outputs a digital high or low based on which input is greater

concepts.label: Voltage Regulator
concepts.definition: Circuit that maintains a constant output voltage regardless of input voltage or load changes

concepts.label: Linear Voltage Regulator
concepts.definition: Regulator using a pass element in linear mode (e.g., 7805) with dropout voltage limitation

concepts.label: LDO Regulator
concepts.definition: Low-dropout linear regulator requiring minimal input-output voltage difference (<0.5V typical)

concepts.label: Switching Regulator
concepts.definition: Voltage regulator using high-frequency switching and energy storage for high-efficiency conversion

concepts.label: Buck Converter
concepts.definition: Step-down DC-DC switching converter that reduces voltage with high efficiency

concepts.label: Boost Converter
concepts.definition: Step-up DC-DC switching converter that increases voltage with high efficiency

concepts.label: Buck-Boost Converter
concepts.definition: DC-DC converter that can step voltage up or down depending on duty cycle

concepts.label: SEPIC Converter
concepts.definition: Single-ended primary inductor converter providing non-inverted buck-boost conversion

concepts.label: Ćuk Converter
concepts.definition: DC-DC converter using capacitive energy transfer providing inverted buck-boost output

concepts.label: Flyback Converter
concepts.definition: Isolated DC-DC converter topology using coupled inductor for multiple outputs

concepts.label: Forward Converter
concepts.definition: Isolated DC-DC converter using transformer with unidirectional core magnetization

concepts.label: Half-Bridge Converter
concepts.definition: DC-DC converter using two switches alternately driving a transformer

concepts.label: Full-Bridge Converter
concepts.definition: DC-DC converter using four switches for high-power isolated conversion

concepts.label: LLC Resonant Converter
concepts.definition: Resonant DC-DC converter using inductor-inductor-capacitor tank for soft switching and high efficiency

concepts.label: Charge Pump
concepts.definition: Switched-capacitor DC-DC converter using capacitors to multiply or invert voltage without inductors

concepts.label: Relay
concepts.definition: Electrically operated switch using electromagnetic coil to mechanically open or close contacts

concepts.label: Reed Relay
concepts.definition: Relay using reed switch contacts sealed in glass tube, activated by external coil

concepts.label: Solid-State Relay
concepts.definition: Relay using semiconductor switching (MOSFET/TRIAC) with no moving parts, offering fast switching

concepts.label: Latching Relay
concepts.definition: Relay that maintains its contact position after coil power is removed, requiring reverse pulse to reset

concepts.label: Contactor
concepts.definition: Heavy-duty relay designed for switching high-current loads in industrial power systems

concepts.label: Crystal Oscillator
concepts.definition: Frequency reference using piezoelectric quartz crystal resonance for precise clock generation

concepts.label: MEMS Oscillator
concepts.definition: Silicon MEMS resonator-based oscillator offering small size and shock resistance vs quartz

concepts.label: TCXO
concepts.definition: Temperature-compensated crystal oscillator maintaining tight frequency stability over temperature

concepts.label: OCXO
concepts.definition: Oven-controlled crystal oscillator holding crystal at constant temperature for ultra-stable frequency

concepts.label: VCXO
concepts.definition: Voltage-controlled crystal oscillator allowing fine frequency adjustment via control voltage

concepts.label: PLL
concepts.definition: Phase-locked loop circuit that synchronizes output frequency and phase to a reference signal

concepts.label: VCO
concepts.definition: Voltage-controlled oscillator whose output frequency varies with input control voltage

concepts.label: 555 Timer
concepts.definition: Versatile IC (NE555) capable of monostable, astable, and bistable timing operations

concepts.label: Fuse
concepts.definition: Overcurrent protection device with a metal element that melts to break the circuit

concepts.label: PTC Resettable Fuse
concepts.definition: Polymer PTC device (polyfuse) that increases resistance when overheated, self-resetting after cooling

concepts.label: Circuit Breaker
concepts.definition: Reusable overcurrent protection device that opens a circuit via electromechanical mechanism

concepts.label: Optocoupler
concepts.definition: Component using LED and phototransistor for electrically isolated signal transfer

concepts.label: Optoisolator
concepts.definition: Device providing galvanic isolation using optical coupling between input and output

concepts.label: Digital Isolator
concepts.definition: IC using capacitive or magnetic coupling for high-speed galvanic isolation of digital signals

concepts.label: Voltage Reference
concepts.definition: Precision IC or circuit providing a stable, accurate voltage for ADC/DAC and measurement systems

concepts.label: Bandgap Reference
concepts.definition: Voltage reference circuit exploiting silicon bandgap energy (~1.25V) for temperature-stable output

concepts.label: Current Source
concepts.definition: Circuit that delivers constant current regardless of load impedance changes

concepts.label: Current Mirror
concepts.definition: Circuit that copies the current flowing in one branch to another, fundamental analog building block

concepts.label: H-Bridge
concepts.definition: Four-switch circuit allowing bidirectional current control through a load, used for DC motor driving

concepts.label: Motor Driver IC
concepts.definition: Integrated circuit for controlling DC, stepper, or brushless motors with built-in protection

concepts.label: Gate Driver
concepts.definition: IC that provides high-current drive signals to switch power MOSFETs and IGBTs rapidly

concepts.label: Level Shifter
concepts.definition: Circuit or IC that translates logic signals between different voltage domains (e.g., 3.3V to 5V)

concepts.label: Bus Transceiver
concepts.definition: IC for bidirectional communication on data buses with enable control

concepts.label: Multiplexer
concepts.definition: IC that selects one of several input signals and routes it to a single output

concepts.label: Demultiplexer
concepts.definition: IC that routes a single input signal to one of several outputs based on selection lines

concepts.label: Encoder IC
concepts.definition: Digital IC that converts 2^n input lines to n-bit binary output code

concepts.label: Decoder IC
concepts.definition: Digital IC that converts n-bit binary input to one of 2^n output lines

concepts.label: Flip-Flop
concepts.definition: Bistable digital circuit storing one bit of data, triggered by clock edge (D, JK, T, SR types)

concepts.label: Latch
concepts.definition: Level-sensitive bistable circuit that stores one bit while enable is active

concepts.label: Shift Register
concepts.definition: Digital circuit of cascaded flip-flops that shifts data serially or in parallel (e.g., 74HC595)

concepts.label: Counter IC
concepts.definition: Digital IC that counts clock pulses in binary or decade sequence (e.g., 74HC4040)

concepts.label: Logic Gate
concepts.definition: Fundamental digital circuit performing boolean operations (AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR)

concepts.label: AND Gate
concepts.definition: Logic gate outputting high only when all inputs are high

concepts.label: OR Gate
concepts.definition: Logic gate outputting high when at least one input is high

concepts.label: NOT Gate
concepts.definition: Inverter gate that outputs the complement of its input

concepts.label: NAND Gate
concepts.definition: Universal logic gate outputting low only when all inputs are high

concepts.label: NOR Gate
concepts.definition: Universal logic gate outputting high only when all inputs are low

concepts.label: XOR Gate
concepts.definition: Exclusive-OR gate outputting high when inputs differ

concepts.label: XNOR Gate
concepts.definition: Exclusive-NOR gate outputting high when inputs are the same

concepts.label: Buffer IC
concepts.definition: Logic IC that provides signal buffering and increased drive current without logic inversion

concepts.label: Schmitt Trigger
concepts.definition: Comparator circuit with hysteresis providing clean digital output from noisy analog signals

concepts.label: Analog Switch
concepts.definition: IC using MOSFET transmission gates to pass analog signals under digital control

concepts.label: Sample-and-Hold
concepts.definition: Circuit that captures and holds an analog voltage at a specific instant for ADC conversion

concepts.label: ADC
concepts.definition: Analog-to-digital converter that transforms continuous analog signals into discrete digital values

concepts.label: SAR ADC
concepts.definition: Successive approximation register ADC using binary search for medium-speed conversion

concepts.label: Sigma-Delta ADC
concepts.definition: Oversampling ADC using noise shaping for high-resolution low-speed conversion

concepts.label: Flash ADC
concepts.definition: Parallel comparator ADC providing the fastest conversion but exponential component count

concepts.label: Pipeline ADC
concepts.definition: Multi-stage ADC combining speed and resolution for video and communications

concepts.label: DAC
concepts.definition: Digital-to-analog converter that transforms digital values into continuous analog voltage or current

concepts.label: R-2R DAC
concepts.definition: DAC using resistor ladder network for simple and accurate digital-to-analog conversion

concepts.label: PWM DAC
concepts.definition: Simple DAC using pulse-width modulation filtered to produce analog output

concepts.label: Microprocessor
concepts.definition: IC containing CPU for executing stored programs, requiring external memory and peripherals

concepts.label: Microcontroller
concepts.definition: Single-chip computer integrating CPU, memory (Flash/RAM), and peripherals for embedded applications

concepts.label: 8-Bit Microcontroller
concepts.definition: MCU with 8-bit data bus, e.g., ATmega328 (Arduino), PIC16, 8051 family

concepts.label: 16-Bit Microcontroller
concepts.definition: MCU with 16-bit data bus, e.g., MSP430, PIC24, dsPIC families

concepts.label: 32-Bit Microcontroller
concepts.definition: MCU with 32-bit data bus, e.g., ARM Cortex-M series (STM32, ESP32, nRF52)

concepts.label: ARM Cortex-M0
concepts.definition: Smallest ARM processor core for ultra-low-power microcontrollers

concepts.label: ARM Cortex-M3
concepts.definition: Mid-range ARM processor core with hardware multiply and divide for general MCU use

concepts.label: ARM Cortex-M4
concepts.definition: ARM core with DSP instructions and optional FPU for signal processing in MCUs

concepts.label: ARM Cortex-M7
concepts.definition: High-performance ARM MCU core with cache and double-precision FPU

concepts.label: ARM Cortex-M33
concepts.definition: ARMv8-M core with TrustZone security for IoT applications

concepts.label: ARM Cortex-A
concepts.definition: ARM application processor family for smartphones, tablets, and high-performance embedded systems

concepts.label: ARM Cortex-R
concepts.definition: ARM real-time processor family for safety-critical applications (automotive, storage)

concepts.label: RISC-V
concepts.definition: Open-source RISC instruction set architecture enabling royalty-free processor design

concepts.label: AVR
concepts.definition: 8-bit RISC microcontroller architecture by Atmel/Microchip, used in Arduino boards

concepts.label: PIC Microcontroller
concepts.definition: Microchip's family of microcontrollers ranging from 8-bit PIC10 to 32-bit PIC32
Classic Intel 8-bit MCU architecture still widely used in simple embedded applications

concepts.label: ESP32
concepts.definition: Espressif dual-core 32-bit MCU with integrated WiFi and Bluetooth for IoT applications

concepts.label: ESP8266
concepts.definition: Espressif low-cost WiFi SoC/MCU widely used for IoT connectivity

concepts.label: STM32
concepts.definition: ST Microelectronics family of ARM Cortex-M based microcontrollers

concepts.label: nRF52
concepts.definition: Nordic Semiconductor BLE SoC family based on ARM Cortex-M4 for wireless IoT

concepts.label: RP2040
concepts.definition: Raspberry Pi dual-core ARM Cortex-M0+ microcontroller with PIO state machines

concepts.label: ATmega328
concepts.definition: Atmel/Microchip 8-bit AVR MCU, the processor in Arduino Uno

concepts.label: DSP
concepts.definition: Digital signal processor optimized for real-time mathematical operations on streaming data

concepts.label: FPGA
concepts.definition: Field-programmable gate array: IC with configurable logic blocks and routing for custom digital circuits

concepts.label: CPLD
concepts.definition: Complex programmable logic device combining multiple PLDs with deterministic timing

concepts.label: ASIC
concepts.definition: Application-specific integrated circuit designed for a particular function, not reprogrammable

concepts.label: SoC
concepts.definition: System on chip integrating CPU, GPU, memory controller, and peripherals on a single die

concepts.label: SiP
concepts.definition: System in package containing multiple dies in a single IC package

concepts.label: GPU
concepts.definition: Graphics processing unit optimized for parallel processing of graphics and compute workloads

concepts.label: NPU
concepts.definition: Neural processing unit optimized for machine learning inference operations

concepts.label: TPU
concepts.definition: Tensor processing unit designed by Google for accelerating neural network computations

concepts.label: EEPROM
concepts.definition: Electrically erasable programmable read-only memory, byte-erasable non-volatile storage

concepts.label: Flash Memory
concepts.definition: Non-volatile semiconductor memory that can be electrically erased in blocks

concepts.label: NOR Flash
concepts.definition: Flash memory with random-access read capability, used for code storage (execute-in-place)

concepts.label: NAND Flash
concepts.definition: Flash memory optimized for sequential access and high density, used in SSDs and SD cards

concepts.label: SRAM
concepts.definition: Static random-access memory using flip-flops, fast but lower density than DRAM

concepts.label: DRAM
concepts.definition: Dynamic random-access memory storing bits as charges in capacitors, requiring periodic refresh

concepts.label: DDR SDRAM
concepts.definition: Double data rate synchronous DRAM transferring data on both clock edges

concepts.label: DDR4
concepts.definition: Fourth generation DDR memory standard operating at 1.2V with speeds up to 3200 MT/s

concepts.label: DDR5
concepts.definition: Fifth generation DDR memory with on-die ECC, dual channels, and speeds up to 8400 MT/s

concepts.label: LPDDR
concepts.definition: Low-power DDR DRAM designed for mobile and embedded systems with reduced voltage

concepts.label: HBM
concepts.definition: High bandwidth memory using 3D-stacked DRAM with through-silicon vias for GPU/AI accelerators

concepts.label: MRAM
concepts.definition: Magnetoresistive RAM storing data using magnetic tunnel junctions, non-volatile with SRAM speed

concepts.label: FRAM
concepts.definition: Ferroelectric RAM using ferroelectric material for fast non-volatile byte-level access

concepts.label: ReRAM
concepts.definition: Resistive RAM storing data by changing resistance of a dielectric material

concepts.label: ROM
concepts.definition: Read-only memory with data permanently programmed during manufacturing

concepts.label: EPROM
concepts.definition: Erasable programmable ROM that can be erased by UV light exposure

concepts.label: OTP Memory
concepts.definition: One-time programmable memory that can be written only once

concepts.label: SD Card
concepts.definition: Secure Digital flash memory card standard for portable storage (SD, SDHC, SDXC)

concepts.label: eMMC
concepts.definition: Embedded MultiMediaCard flash storage integrating controller and NAND in BGA package for mobile devices

concepts.label: UFS
concepts.definition: Universal Flash Storage standard providing faster serial interface than eMMC for mobile devices

concepts.label: Clock Generator
concepts.definition: IC that generates clock signals from a reference, often with PLL for multiple output frequencies

concepts.label: RTC
concepts.definition: Real-time clock IC maintaining time/date with battery backup for embedded systems

concepts.label: Watchdog Timer
concepts.definition: Timer circuit that resets a microcontroller if software fails to periodically service it

concepts.label: DMA Controller
concepts.definition: Hardware that transfers data between memory and peripherals without CPU involvement

concepts.label: Interrupt Controller
concepts.definition: Hardware managing multiple interrupt sources and prioritization for a processor

concepts.label: Power Management IC
concepts.definition: Integrated circuit managing power rails, sequencing, and monitoring in electronic systems

concepts.label: Battery Management System
concepts.definition: Circuit/IC monitoring battery voltage, current, temperature, and state of charge for safe operation

concepts.label: Battery Charger IC
concepts.definition: IC implementing charging algorithms (CC/CV) for lithium, lead-acid, or NiMH batteries

concepts.label: USB Power Delivery Controller
concepts.definition: IC implementing USB PD protocol for negotiating higher voltage/current over USB-C

concepts.label: POE Controller
concepts.definition: IC implementing Power over Ethernet for powering devices through Ethernet cables

concepts.label: WiFi Module
concepts.definition: Integrated wireless module implementing IEEE 802.11 standards for wireless LAN connectivity

concepts.label: Bluetooth Module
concepts.definition: Wireless module implementing Bluetooth Classic and/or BLE for short-range communication

concepts.label: LoRa Module
concepts.definition: Long-range low-power wireless module using chirp spread spectrum for IoT networks

concepts.label: Zigbee Module
concepts.definition: IEEE 802.15.4-based wireless module for low-power mesh networking in home automation

concepts.label: Thread Module
concepts.definition: IPv6-based mesh networking module for IoT, underlying protocol for Matter

concepts.label: Z-Wave Module
concepts.definition: Sub-GHz wireless module for home automation with interoperability certification

concepts.label: NFC IC
concepts.definition: Near-field communication IC for contactless data exchange at 13.56 MHz

concepts.label: RFID Tag
concepts.definition: Radio-frequency identification transponder using passive or active RF communication

concepts.label: GPS Module
concepts.definition: GNSS receiver module determining position using satellite signals (GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou)

concepts.label: Cellular Modem
concepts.definition: Module providing 2G/3G/4G/5G cellular connectivity for IoT and mobile applications

concepts.label: UWB Module
concepts.definition: Ultra-wideband radio module for precise ranging and positioning at centimeter accuracy

concepts.label: SigFox Module
concepts.definition: LPWAN module using ultra-narrowband for low-throughput IoT communication

concepts.label: NB-IoT Module
concepts.definition: Narrowband IoT cellular module for low-power wide-area network applications

concepts.label: LTE-M Module
concepts.definition: LTE Cat-M1 cellular module optimized for IoT with low power and extended coverage

concepts.label: Temperature Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring temperature using thermocouples, RTDs, thermistors, or semiconductor junctions

concepts.label: Thermocouple
concepts.definition: Temperature sensor using voltage generated at junction of two dissimilar metals (Seebeck effect)

concepts.label: RTD
concepts.definition: Resistance temperature detector using metal (usually platinum) whose resistance changes linearly with temperature

concepts.label: Digital Temperature Sensor
concepts.definition: IC combining temperature sensing and ADC with digital interface (e.g., DS18B20, TMP102)

concepts.label: Infrared Temperature Sensor
concepts.definition: Non-contact temperature sensor measuring infrared radiation emitted by objects (e.g., MLX90614)

concepts.label: Humidity Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring relative humidity using capacitive or resistive sensing elements

concepts.label: Temperature-Humidity Sensor
concepts.definition: Combined sensor for both temperature and humidity (e.g., DHT22, SHT40, BME280)

concepts.label: Pressure Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring gas or liquid pressure using piezoresistive, capacitive, or piezoelectric elements

concepts.label: Barometric Pressure Sensor
concepts.definition: MEMS sensor measuring atmospheric pressure for altitude estimation (e.g., BMP280, LPS22HB)

concepts.label: Differential Pressure Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring pressure difference between two ports

concepts.label: Force Sensor
concepts.definition: Transducer converting mechanical force into an electrical signal (FSR, strain gauge, load cell)

concepts.label: Load Cell
concepts.definition: Force transducer using strain gauges in Wheatstone bridge configuration for weight measurement

concepts.label: Strain Gauge
concepts.definition: Sensor whose resistance changes proportionally to mechanical strain/deformation

concepts.label: Piezoelectric Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor generating voltage from mechanical stress in piezoelectric material (quartz, PZT)

concepts.label: Accelerometer
concepts.definition: MEMS sensor measuring acceleration/tilt along one or more axes (e.g., ADXL345, LIS3DH)

concepts.label: Gyroscope
concepts.definition: MEMS sensor measuring angular velocity/rotation rate around one or more axes

concepts.label: IMU
concepts.definition: Inertial measurement unit combining accelerometer, gyroscope, and optionally magnetometer (e.g., MPU-6050, BMI270)

concepts.label: Magnetometer
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring magnetic field strength and direction (e.g., HMC5883L, QMC5883L)

concepts.label: Hall Effect Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting magnetic field presence and strength via Hall voltage across a conductor

concepts.label: Current Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring electrical current using Hall effect, shunt resistor, or Rogowski coil

concepts.label: Voltage Sensor
concepts.definition: Circuit measuring voltage using resistive dividers or isolated sensing ICs

concepts.label: Proximity Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting nearby objects without contact using IR, ultrasonic, capacitive, or inductive methods

concepts.label: Infrared Proximity Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor using IR LED and photodetector to detect nearby objects by reflected light

concepts.label: Ultrasonic Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring distance using time-of-flight of ultrasonic sound pulses (e.g., HC-SR04)

concepts.label: LiDAR Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring distance using time-of-flight of laser pulses for precise ranging and 3D mapping

concepts.label: ToF Sensor
concepts.definition: Time-of-flight sensor measuring distance by timing light round-trip (e.g., VL53L0X, VL53L1X)

concepts.label: Capacitive Touch Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting touch by measuring capacitance change when a finger approaches an electrode

concepts.label: Resistive Touch Sensor
concepts.definition: Touch sensor using pressure between two conductive layers to determine touch position

concepts.label: PIR Sensor
concepts.definition: Passive infrared sensor detecting motion by sensing changes in infrared radiation from warm bodies

concepts.label: Ambient Light Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring ambient light intensity, often with spectral response matching human eye

concepts.label: UV Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring ultraviolet radiation intensity (e.g., VEML6070, GUVA-S12SD)

concepts.label: Color Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting color by measuring reflected light through RGB filters (e.g., TCS34725)

concepts.label: IR Receiver
concepts.definition: Demodulating infrared receiver for remote control signals (e.g., TSOP38238, VS1838B)

concepts.label: Microphone
concepts.definition: Acoustic transducer converting sound pressure into electrical signals

concepts.label: MEMS Microphone
concepts.definition: Miniature silicon microphone using MEMS diaphragm, common in smartphones and IoT devices

concepts.label: Electret Microphone
concepts.definition: Condenser microphone using permanently charged electret material for bias

concepts.label: Speaker
concepts.definition: Electroacoustic transducer converting electrical signals into sound waves

concepts.label: Piezo Buzzer
concepts.definition: Acoustic transducer using piezoelectric effect to produce alert tones

concepts.label: Gas Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting specific gas concentrations (e.g., MQ series for CO, methane, alcohol, smoke)

concepts.label: CO2 Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring carbon dioxide concentration using NDIR or electrochemical methods

concepts.label: Particulate Matter Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring airborne particle concentration (PM2.5, PM10) using laser scattering

concepts.label: VOC Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting volatile organic compounds for air quality monitoring

concepts.label: Smoke Detector
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting smoke particles using ionization chamber or photoelectric methods

concepts.label: pH Sensor
concepts.definition: Electrochemical sensor measuring hydrogen ion concentration (acidity/alkalinity) of a solution

concepts.label: Dissolved Oxygen Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring oxygen concentration in liquids using electrochemical or optical methods

concepts.label: Turbidity Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring water clarity by detecting scattered or transmitted light through liquid

concepts.label: Conductivity Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring electrical conductivity of a solution for water quality monitoring

concepts.label: Soil Moisture Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring volumetric water content in soil using capacitive or resistive methods

concepts.label: Rain Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting precipitation using conductive or capacitive water detection on exposed surface

concepts.label: Water Level Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring liquid level using float, capacitive, ultrasonic, or pressure-based methods

concepts.label: Flow Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring fluid flow rate using turbine, vortex, ultrasonic, or electromagnetic methods

concepts.label: Tilt Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting angular orientation change, using mercury switch, ball switch, or MEMS

concepts.label: Vibration Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting mechanical vibration using piezoelectric or MEMS accelerometer elements

concepts.label: Flex Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor whose resistance changes when bent, used in wearables and robotics

concepts.label: Rotary Encoder
concepts.definition: Sensor producing digital pulses proportional to shaft rotation angle or speed

concepts.label: Optical Encoder
concepts.definition: Encoder using LED and photodetector with slotted disk for precise position/speed sensing

concepts.label: Linear Encoder
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring linear position using optical or magnetic scale and read head

concepts.label: Potentiometric Position Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring position via wiper movement on resistive element

concepts.label: LVDT
concepts.definition: Linear variable differential transformer measuring linear displacement with high precision

concepts.label: Inductive Proximity Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting metallic objects using eddy current losses in an oscillating magnetic field

concepts.label: Capacitive Proximity Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting objects by measuring capacitance change caused by nearby materials

concepts.label: Radar Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor using radio waves for distance, velocity, and angle measurement of objects

concepts.label: mmWave Radar
concepts.definition: Millimeter-wave radar sensor (60-77 GHz) for gesture recognition, presence detection, and vital signs

concepts.label: Image Sensor
concepts.definition: Semiconductor sensor converting optical images into electronic signals (CCD or CMOS)

concepts.label: CCD Image Sensor
concepts.definition: Charge-coupled device sensor transferring charge packets for high-quality imaging

concepts.label: CMOS Image Sensor
concepts.definition: Image sensor using CMOS technology with per-pixel amplifiers for low power and integration

concepts.label: Thermal Camera Sensor
concepts.definition: Infrared sensor array producing thermal images from heat radiation (e.g., FLIR Lepton, MLX90640)

concepts.label: Fingerprint Sensor
concepts.definition: Biometric sensor capturing fingerprint patterns using capacitive, optical, or ultrasonic methods

concepts.label: Heart Rate Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring pulse rate using photoplethysmography (PPG) with LED and photodetector

concepts.label: SpO2 Sensor
concepts.definition: Pulse oximetry sensor measuring blood oxygen saturation using red and infrared LEDs

concepts.label: ECG Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring heart electrical activity using surface electrodes (e.g., AD8232, MAX30003)

concepts.label: EMG Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring electrical activity of muscles for biomedical and prosthetic applications

concepts.label: EEG Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring brain electrical activity through scalp electrodes

concepts.label: Blood Pressure Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring arterial blood pressure using oscillometric or tonometric methods

concepts.label: GSR Sensor
concepts.definition: Galvanic skin response sensor measuring skin conductance for stress and emotion detection

concepts.label: Geiger-Muller Tube
concepts.definition: Radiation detector sensing ionizing radiation (alpha, beta, gamma) via gas ionization

concepts.label: Photoelectric Sensor
concepts.definition: Industrial sensor using light beam for object detection (through-beam, retro-reflective, diffuse)

concepts.label: Limit Switch
concepts.definition: Mechanical sensor detecting physical position by actuating switch contacts at end of travel

concepts.label: Reed Switch
concepts.definition: Magnetically actuated switch with ferromagnetic reed contacts sealed in glass envelope

concepts.label: Optical Interrupter
concepts.definition: Sensor with LED and phototransistor in slot configuration detecting object presence/absence

concepts.label: Weight Sensor Module
concepts.definition: Load cell with amplifier (HX711) for digital weight measurement in scales

concepts.label: Compass Module
concepts.definition: Digital magnetometer module providing heading/bearing information

concepts.label: Inclinometer
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring tilt angle relative to gravity using MEMS or fluid-based elements

concepts.label: Wind Speed Sensor
concepts.definition: Anemometer measuring wind speed using cup rotation, hot-wire, or ultrasonic methods

concepts.label: Wind Direction Sensor
concepts.definition: Wind vane sensor providing wind direction data as analog or digital output

concepts.label: Lightning Detector
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting electromagnetic signatures of lightning strikes (e.g., AS3935)

concepts.label: Gesture Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor detecting hand gestures using IR proximity or ToF arrays (e.g., APDS-9960)

concepts.label: Magnetic Encoder
concepts.definition: Encoder using magnetoresistive or Hall elements to sense rotation of magnetic target

concepts.label: WiFi

concepts.label: 100G Ethernet

concepts.label: Ethernet MAC

concepts.label: RS-422

concepts.label: I2S

concepts.label: TDM

concepts.label: M.2

concepts.label: SDIO

concepts.label: JTAG

concepts.label: SWD

concepts.label: MQTT

concepts.label: CoAP

concepts.label: OPC UA
concepts.definition: Open Platform Communications Unified Architecture for industrial automation interoperability

concepts.label: EtherCAT

concepts.label: PROFINET

concepts.label: CANopen

concepts.label: PROFIBUS

concepts.label: HART Protocol

concepts.label: DMX512

concepts.label: DALI

concepts.label: KNX

concepts.label: LoRaWAN

concepts.label: Satellite IoT
