concepts.definition: Lithium titanate anode battery with extreme cycle life (>10,000) and fast charging at 2.4V

concepts.label: Lithium-Sulfur Battery
concepts.definition: Next-gen battery using sulfur cathode promising 2-3x energy density over Li-ion

concepts.label: Solid-State Battery
concepts.definition: Battery using solid electrolyte instead of liquid, promising higher energy density and safety

concepts.label: Sodium-Ion Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery using sodium intercalation as low-cost alternative to lithium-ion

concepts.label: Lead-Acid Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery using lead plates and sulfuric acid, 2V/cell, used in automotive/UPS

concepts.label: AGM Battery
concepts.definition: Absorbed glass mat lead-acid battery with electrolyte absorbed in fiberglass, sealed maintenance-free

concepts.label: Gel Battery
concepts.definition: Lead-acid battery using gelled electrolyte for spill-proof sealed operation

concepts.label: NiMH Battery
concepts.definition: Nickel-metal hydride rechargeable battery, 1.2V/cell, higher capacity than NiCd

concepts.label: NiCd Battery
concepts.definition: Nickel-cadmium rechargeable battery, 1.2V/cell, robust but toxic cadmium content

concepts.label: Alkaline Battery
concepts.definition: Primary (non-rechargeable) battery using zinc-manganese dioxide chemistry, 1.5V

concepts.label: Zinc-Carbon Battery
concepts.definition: Low-cost primary battery with shorter life than alkaline, 1.5V

concepts.label: Lithium Primary Battery
concepts.definition: Non-rechargeable lithium battery (CR2032, CR123A) with 3V and long shelf life (10+ years)

concepts.label: Silver Oxide Battery
concepts.definition: Primary button cell (1.55V) with flat discharge curve, used in watches

concepts.label: Zinc-Air Battery
concepts.definition: Battery using oxygen from air as cathode reactant, high energy density for hearing aids

concepts.label: Flow Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery storing energy in external electrolyte tanks (vanadium, zinc-bromine)

concepts.label: Vanadium Redox Battery
concepts.definition: Flow battery using vanadium electrolyte in both half-cells for grid-scale storage

concepts.label: Aluminum-Air Battery
concepts.definition: High energy density primary battery using aluminum anode oxidation

concepts.label: Iron-Air Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery using iron anode and air cathode for grid-scale storage

concepts.label: Thin Film Battery
concepts.definition: Solid-state micro-battery deposited as thin films for powering MEMS and smart cards

concepts.label: Nuclear Battery
concepts.definition: Radioisotope thermoelectric generator or betavoltaic cell converting radioactive decay to electricity

concepts.label: Lithium Thionyl Chloride Battery
concepts.definition: Primary lithium battery with highest energy density (3.6V) for long-life industrial/military use

concepts.label: Nickel-Zinc Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery with 1.6V cell voltage as NiMH alternative with higher voltage

concepts.label: Nickel-Iron Battery
concepts.definition: Very durable rechargeable battery (Edison cell) with long cycle life but lower efficiency

concepts.label: Energy Harvesting
concepts.definition: Capturing ambient energy (solar, thermal, vibration, RF) to power low-power electronics

concepts.label: Solar Cell
concepts.definition: Photovoltaic device converting light directly into electricity via semiconductor p-n junction

concepts.label: Thermoelectric Generator
concepts.definition: Device converting temperature differential to electricity via Seebeck effect (Peltier module in reverse)

concepts.label: Piezoelectric Energy Harvester
concepts.definition: Device converting mechanical vibration to electricity using piezoelectric material

concepts.label: Silicon
concepts.definition: Element (Si, Z=14) and primary semiconductor material for ICs, solar cells, and MEMS

concepts.label: Germanium
concepts.definition: Semiconductor element (Ge) used historically in transistors, now in SiGe heterojunctions and IR optics

concepts.label: Gallium Arsenide
concepts.definition: III-V compound semiconductor (GaAs) for high-frequency, optoelectronic, and solar cell applications

concepts.label: Gallium Nitride
concepts.definition: Wide-bandgap III-V semiconductor (GaN) for power electronics, RF amplifiers, and LEDs

concepts.label: Silicon Carbide
concepts.definition: Wide-bandgap semiconductor (SiC) for high-voltage high-temperature power devices

concepts.label: Indium Phosphide
concepts.definition: III-V semiconductor (InP) for optical communication and high-speed electronics

concepts.label: Indium Gallium Arsenide
concepts.definition: Ternary III-V alloy (InGaAs) for infrared photodetectors and high-speed transistors

concepts.label: Aluminum Gallium Arsenide
concepts.definition: Ternary III-V alloy (AlGaAs) for heterojunction lasers and HEMTs

concepts.label: Silicon-Germanium
concepts.definition: SiGe alloy enabling high-speed bipolar transistors on silicon substrates

concepts.label: Gallium Oxide
concepts.definition: Ultra-wide bandgap semiconductor (Ga2O3) for next-gen high-voltage power devices

concepts.label: Diamond Semiconductor
concepts.definition: Wide-bandgap semiconductor with extreme thermal conductivity and breakdown voltage potential

concepts.label: Zinc Oxide
concepts.definition: II-VI semiconductor (ZnO) used in varistors, piezoelectric devices, and transparent electronics

concepts.label: Cadmium Telluride
concepts.definition: II-VI semiconductor (CdTe) used in thin-film solar cells and radiation detectors

concepts.label: CIGS
concepts.definition: Copper indium gallium selenide thin-film solar cell material with high efficiency potential

concepts.label: Perovskite
concepts.definition: Crystal structure materials (e.g., methylammonium lead halide) for next-gen solar cells

concepts.label: N-Type Semiconductor
concepts.definition: Semiconductor doped with donor impurities providing excess electrons as majority carriers

concepts.label: P-Type Semiconductor
concepts.definition: Semiconductor doped with acceptor impurities providing holes as majority carriers

concepts.label: PN Junction
concepts.definition: Interface between p-type and n-type semiconductor regions forming the basis of diodes

concepts.label: Depletion Region
concepts.definition: Zone at p-n junction depleted of mobile carriers, creating built-in potential barrier

concepts.label: Bandgap
concepts.definition: Energy difference between valence and conduction bands in a semiconductor, determining electrical properties

concepts.label: Doping
concepts.definition: Intentional introduction of impurity atoms into semiconductor to modify electrical conductivity

concepts.label: Epitaxy
concepts.definition: Crystal growth technique depositing thin semiconductor layers with controlled composition on substrate

concepts.label: MBE
concepts.definition: Molecular beam epitaxy: ultra-high-vacuum technique for growing precise semiconductor heterostructures

concepts.label: MOCVD
concepts.definition: Metal-organic chemical vapor deposition for growing III-V and nitride semiconductor films

concepts.label: CVD
concepts.definition: Chemical vapor deposition process for depositing thin films from gas-phase chemical reactions

concepts.label: PVD
concepts.definition: Physical vapor deposition using sputtering or evaporation for thin metal and dielectric films

concepts.label: Photolithography
concepts.definition: Process using UV light and photoresist to transfer circuit patterns onto semiconductor wafers

concepts.label: EUV Lithography
concepts.definition: Extreme ultraviolet lithography using 13.5nm light for sub-7nm semiconductor node patterning

concepts.label: Etching
concepts.definition: Process removing material from wafer surface in patterned areas (wet chemical or dry plasma)

concepts.label: Ion Implantation
concepts.definition: Process accelerating dopant ions into semiconductor wafer for precise doping control

concepts.label: Oxidation
concepts.definition: Growing silicon dioxide layer on silicon wafer for gate dielectric and isolation

concepts.label: Diffusion
concepts.definition: High-temperature process spreading dopant atoms into semiconductor from surface source

concepts.label: CMP
concepts.definition: Chemical mechanical planarization polishing wafer surface flat between process steps

concepts.label: Wafer
concepts.definition: Thin circular slice of semiconductor crystal (usually silicon) serving as substrate for IC fabrication

concepts.label: Die
concepts.definition: Individual integrated circuit chip cut from a processed semiconductor wafer

concepts.label: Wire Bonding
concepts.definition: Process connecting IC die pads to package leads using thin gold or aluminum wires

concepts.label: Flip Chip
concepts.definition: Die attachment method using solder bumps facing down for shorter interconnects and better thermal performance

concepts.label: BGA Package
concepts.definition: Ball grid array IC package using solder balls on bottom for high pin count and good thermal path

concepts.label: QFP Package
concepts.definition: Quad flat package IC with leads on all four sides for medium pin count applications

concepts.label: QFN Package
concepts.definition: Quad flat no-lead package with exposed pad for good thermal performance in small size

concepts.label: SOIC Package
concepts.definition: Small outline integrated circuit package with gull-wing leads on two sides

concepts.label: DIP Package
concepts.definition: Dual in-line package with through-hole pins, used for prototyping and legacy designs

concepts.label: SOT-23 Package
concepts.definition: Small outline transistor package with 3-6 pins for discrete semiconductors

concepts.label: TO-220 Package
concepts.definition: Through-hole power package with tab for heatsink mounting, rated for several watts

concepts.label: TO-92 Package
concepts.definition: Small through-hole package for low-power transistors and sensors

concepts.label: WLCSP
concepts.definition: Wafer-level chip scale package: bare die size package with solder bumps, no substrate

concepts.label: CSP
concepts.definition: Chip scale package with area no more than 1.2x die size for compact assembly

concepts.label: MCM
concepts.definition: Multi-chip module packaging multiple dies in single package for system integration

concepts.label: Through-Silicon Via
concepts.definition: TSV: vertical electrical connection through silicon die for 3D IC stacking

concepts.label: Chiplet
concepts.definition: Small modular die designed to be integrated with other chiplets in an advanced package

concepts.label: PCB
concepts.definition: Printed circuit board: substrate with copper traces providing mechanical support and electrical connections

concepts.label: Single-Layer PCB
concepts.definition: PCB with copper traces on one side only, lowest cost for simple circuits

concepts.label: Double-Layer PCB
concepts.definition: PCB with copper on both sides connected by vias

concepts.label: Multi-Layer PCB
concepts.definition: PCB with 4+ copper layers for complex designs with dedicated power/ground planes

concepts.label: HDI PCB
concepts.definition: High-density interconnect PCB with microvias, fine traces, and high component density

concepts.label: Flex PCB
concepts.definition: Flexible printed circuit using polyimide substrate for bendable and space-constrained applications

concepts.label: Rigid-Flex PCB
concepts.definition: PCB combining rigid and flexible sections in one assembly for 3D packaging

concepts.label: FR-4
concepts.definition: Flame-retardant glass-reinforced epoxy laminate, the standard PCB substrate material

concepts.label: Rogers Laminate
concepts.definition: High-frequency PCB substrate material with controlled dielectric constant for RF applications

concepts.label: Polyimide Substrate
concepts.definition: Flexible high-temperature PCB substrate material (Kapton) for flex circuits

concepts.label: Copper Pour
concepts.definition: Large area of copper fill on PCB for ground plane, power distribution, and thermal relief

concepts.label: Via
concepts.definition: Plated hole connecting copper layers in a PCB, available as through-hole, blind, or buried

concepts.label: Through-Hole Via
concepts.definition: Via penetrating entire PCB stackup connecting all layers

concepts.label: Blind Via
concepts.definition: Via connecting outer layer to inner layer without penetrating entire PCB

concepts.label: Buried Via
concepts.definition: Via connecting only inner layers without reaching either surface of PCB

concepts.label: Microvia
concepts.definition: Small laser-drilled via (<150μm) in HDI PCBs connecting adjacent layers

concepts.label: Solder Mask
concepts.definition: Protective coating on PCB preventing solder from bridging between traces and pads

concepts.label: Silkscreen
concepts.definition: Printed text and symbols on PCB for component identification and assembly guidance

concepts.label: Surface Finish
concepts.definition: PCB pad coating (HASL, ENIG, OSP, Immersion Tin/Silver) protecting copper for soldering

concepts.label: HASL
concepts.definition: Hot Air Solder Leveling: PCB finish applying molten solder then leveling with hot air

concepts.label: ENIG
concepts.definition: Electroless Nickel Immersion Gold: PCB finish providing flat solderable surface for fine-pitch

concepts.label: OSP
concepts.definition: Organic Solderability Preservative: thin organic coating protecting copper pads on PCB

concepts.label: Immersion Silver
concepts.definition: PCB surface finish depositing thin silver layer for good solderability and planarity

concepts.label: Immersion Tin
concepts.definition: PCB finish depositing tin layer, good for press-fit and backplane applications

concepts.label: Solder Paste
concepts.definition: Mixture of solder alloy powder and flux used for SMT reflow assembly

concepts.label: Stencil
concepts.definition: Thin metal sheet with laser-cut apertures for applying solder paste to PCB pads

concepts.label: Reflow Soldering
concepts.definition: SMT assembly process melting solder paste in oven with controlled temperature profile

concepts.label: Wave Soldering
concepts.definition: Through-hole assembly process passing PCB over a wave of molten solder

concepts.label: Hand Soldering
concepts.definition: Manual soldering using soldering iron for prototyping, rework, and through-hole assembly

concepts.label: SMT
concepts.definition: Surface mount technology: component mounting directly on PCB surface without through-holes

concepts.label: Through-Hole Technology
concepts.definition: Component mounting using leads inserted through PCB holes and soldered on opposite side

concepts.label: Pick and Place
concepts.definition: Automated machine placing SMD components onto solder paste on PCB during assembly

concepts.label: AOI
concepts.definition: Automated optical inspection using cameras to check solder joints and component placement

concepts.label: X-Ray Inspection
concepts.definition: Using X-rays to inspect hidden solder joints under BGA and QFN packages

concepts.label: ICT
concepts.definition: In-circuit test using bed-of-nails fixture to verify component values and connections on assembled PCB

concepts.label: Flying Probe Test
concepts.definition: PCB test using movable probes for electrical verification without custom fixture

concepts.label: Functional Test
concepts.definition: Testing assembled PCB by powering it and verifying correct operation of the complete circuit

concepts.label: DFM
concepts.definition: Design for manufacturability: PCB design practices ensuring reliable and cost-effective fabrication

concepts.label: DFA
concepts.definition: Design for assembly: optimizing PCB layout for efficient and error-free component placement

concepts.label: DFT
concepts.definition: Design for testability: incorporating test points and structures for production testing

concepts.label: Panelization
concepts.definition: Arranging multiple PCB copies on a panel for efficient manufacturing and assembly

concepts.label: V-Score
concepts.definition: V-shaped groove cut partially through PCB panel for breaking apart individual boards

concepts.label: Tab Routing
concepts.definition: PCB panel separation method using routed perforations (mouse bites) between boards

concepts.label: Impedance Control
concepts.definition: PCB manufacturing maintaining specific trace impedance (50Ω, 100Ω differential) for signal integrity

concepts.label: Controlled Dielectric
concepts.definition: PCB laminate with specified dielectric constant and thickness for impedance-controlled designs

concepts.label: Castellated Holes
concepts.definition: Half-moon plated holes on PCB edge for soldering module directly to carrier board

concepts.label: Edge Plating
concepts.definition: Copper plating on PCB edges for grounding or board-to-board connections

concepts.label: Gold Fingers
concepts.definition: Gold-plated edge connector pads on PCB for insertion into card edge connector

concepts.label: Conformal Coating
concepts.definition: Protective polymer coating applied to assembled PCB for moisture, dust, and chemical resistance

concepts.label: Potting
concepts.definition: Encapsulating electronics in resin compound for protection against shock, vibration, and environment

concepts.label: Thermal Via
concepts.definition: Via array under power components to transfer heat to inner copper planes or opposite side

concepts.label: Heatsink
concepts.definition: Passive thermal management component increasing surface area for convective heat dissipation

concepts.label: Thermal Pad
concepts.definition: Thermally conductive interface material filling gap between component and heatsink

concepts.label: Thermal Paste
concepts.definition: Thermally conductive compound applied between IC and heatsink to minimize thermal resistance

concepts.label: Fan
concepts.definition: Active cooling device moving air over heatsink or PCB for enhanced heat dissipation

concepts.label: Peltier Module
concepts.definition: Thermoelectric cooler/heater transferring heat using Peltier effect in bismuth telluride junctions

concepts.label: Heat Pipe
concepts.definition: Sealed copper tube using phase-change fluid for efficient heat transfer over distance

concepts.label: Vapor Chamber
concepts.definition: Flat heat pipe spreading heat in two dimensions for uniform thermal distribution

concepts.label: Gerber File
concepts.definition: Standard file format (RS-274X) defining PCB copper layers, masks, and drills for manufacturing

concepts.label: Drill File
concepts.definition: Excellon format file specifying hole locations and sizes for PCB drilling

concepts.label: BOM
concepts.definition: Bill of materials listing all components, quantities, and designators for PCB assembly

concepts.label: CPL
concepts.definition: Component placement list (pick-and-place file) with component XY coordinates and rotation

concepts.label: Netlist
concepts.definition: List of electrical connections defining how components are wired together in a circuit

concepts.label: Schematic
concepts.definition: Circuit diagram showing components and their electrical connections using standard symbols

concepts.label: EDA
concepts.definition: Electronic design automation software tools for schematic capture, PCB layout, and simulation

concepts.label: KiCad
concepts.definition: Open-source EDA suite for schematic capture and PCB layout design

concepts.label: Altium Designer
concepts.definition: Professional EDA software for PCB design with unified schematic-layout environment

concepts.label: Eagle
concepts.definition: Autodesk PCB design software popular for hobbyist and small-scale professional designs

concepts.label: OrCAD
concepts.definition: Cadence EDA suite for schematic capture and PCB layout

concepts.label: SPICE
concepts.definition: Simulation Program with Integrated Circuit Emphasis for analog circuit simulation

concepts.label: LTspice
concepts.definition: Free SPICE simulator from Analog Devices for analog circuit simulation

concepts.label: Signal Integrity
concepts.definition: Quality of electrical signal on PCB trace, affected by impedance, crosstalk, and reflections

concepts.label: Power Integrity
concepts.definition: Quality of power distribution network ensuring clean voltage delivery to all components

concepts.label: EMI
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic interference: unwanted electromagnetic energy affecting circuit performance

concepts.label: EMC
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic compatibility: ability of electronic system to function without causing or suffering EMI

concepts.label: ESD Protection
concepts.definition: Circuits and design practices protecting electronics from electrostatic discharge damage

concepts.label: Decoupling Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor placed near IC power pins to filter high-frequency noise and supply transient current

concepts.label: Bypass Capacitor
concepts.definition: Capacitor providing low-impedance path to ground for AC noise near power pins

concepts.label: Ground Plane
concepts.definition: Continuous copper layer dedicated to ground return path, reducing impedance and EMI

concepts.label: Power Plane
concepts.definition: Continuous copper layer dedicated to power distribution in multi-layer PCB

concepts.label: Differential Pair
concepts.definition: Two PCB traces carrying complementary signals with controlled spacing for noise rejection

concepts.label: Trace Width Calculator
concepts.definition: Tool computing PCB trace width for required current capacity based on IPC-2221 standards

concepts.label: Antenna
concepts.definition: Device converting electrical signals to electromagnetic waves and vice versa

concepts.label: PCB Antenna
concepts.definition: Antenna etched directly on PCB substrate (inverted-F, meander, patch) for wireless devices

concepts.label: Chip Antenna
concepts.definition: Miniature SMD antenna component for Bluetooth, WiFi, and GPS applications

concepts.label: Dipole Antenna
concepts.definition: Half-wave antenna with two conductive elements for omnidirectional radiation pattern

concepts.label: Patch Antenna
concepts.definition: Flat directional antenna using metal patch over ground plane, common in GPS and radar

concepts.label: Yagi Antenna
concepts.definition: Directional antenna using driven element with parasitic directors and reflector

concepts.label: Whip Antenna
concepts.definition: Flexible monopole antenna for omnidirectional reception on portable devices

concepts.label: MIMO Antenna
concepts.definition: Multiple-input multiple-output antenna system using spatial multiplexing for throughput

concepts.label: Connector
concepts.definition: Electromechanical device for joining electrical circuits with detachable interface

concepts.label: Pin Header
concepts.definition: Row of pins at 2.54mm pitch for board-to-board or wire-to-board connections

concepts.label: JST Connector
concepts.definition: Family of compact wire-to-board connectors (PH, XH, SH series) common in electronics

concepts.label: Molex Connector
concepts.definition: Wire-to-board connector family widely used in computers and consumer electronics

concepts.label: FPC Connector
concepts.definition: Flat flexible cable connector for ribbon cables in displays and mobile devices

concepts.label: D-Sub Connector
concepts.definition: D-shaped subminiature connector family (DB9, DB25, VGA) for serial and parallel interfaces

concepts.label: RJ45 Connector
concepts.definition: 8P8C modular connector for Ethernet networking over twisted-pair cable

concepts.label: SMA Connector
concepts.definition: SubMiniature version A coaxial RF connector for frequencies up to 18 GHz

concepts.label: BNC Connector
concepts.definition: Bayonet Neill–Concelman coaxial connector for RF and instrumentation

concepts.label: Barrel Jack
concepts.definition: DC power connector commonly used in embedded systems and consumer electronics (2.1/2.5mm)

concepts.label: Screw Terminal
concepts.definition: Wire connection terminal using screw clamp for secure and removable connections

concepts.label: Spring Terminal
concepts.definition: Push-in wire terminal using spring mechanism for tool-free connection

concepts.label: Banana Plug
concepts.definition: Single-pin connector for test equipment and speaker connections

concepts.label: Test Point
concepts.definition: Exposed pad or via on PCB providing access for probe measurement during testing

concepts.label: Multimeter
concepts.definition: Instrument measuring voltage, current, and resistance (DMM: digital multimeter)

concepts.label: Oscilloscope
concepts.definition: Instrument displaying voltage waveforms over time for signal analysis and debugging

concepts.label: Logic Analyzer
concepts.definition: Instrument capturing and displaying multiple digital signals simultaneously for timing analysis

concepts.label: Spectrum Analyzer
concepts.definition: Instrument displaying signal amplitude versus frequency for RF and EMI analysis

concepts.label: Signal Generator
concepts.definition: Instrument producing electrical waveforms (sine, square, arbitrary) for circuit testing

concepts.label: Power Supply
concepts.definition: Instrument providing regulated DC voltage and current for powering circuits under test

concepts.label: LCR Meter
concepts.definition: Instrument measuring inductance, capacitance, and resistance of components at various frequencies

concepts.label: Network Analyzer
concepts.definition: Instrument measuring S-parameters of RF networks for impedance and transmission characterization

concepts.label: Protocol Analyzer
concepts.definition: Tool capturing and decoding serial communication protocols (I2C, SPI, UART, CAN) for debugging

concepts.label: Thermal Camera
concepts.definition: Imaging tool for visualizing heat distribution on PCBs to identify hotspots and thermal issues

concepts.label: Soldering Iron
concepts.definition: Hand tool with heated tip for melting solder to join electronic components to PCB

concepts.label: Soldering Station
concepts.definition: Temperature-controlled soldering system with iron, stand, and tip selection for precise work

concepts.label: Hot Air Rework Station
concepts.definition: Tool using focused hot air for SMD component removal and replacement

concepts.label: Solder
concepts.definition: Metal alloy (Sn-Pb or lead-free Sn-Ag-Cu) melted to create electrical and mechanical joints

concepts.label: Lead-Free Solder
concepts.definition: RoHS-compliant solder alloy (typically SAC305: Sn96.5/Ag3.0/Cu0.5) with higher melting point

concepts.label: Flux
concepts.definition: Chemical agent cleaning and preventing oxidation on metal surfaces during soldering

concepts.label: Solder Wick
concepts.definition: Braided copper wire with flux for removing excess solder by capillary action

concepts.label: Desoldering Pump
concepts.definition: Vacuum tool for removing molten solder from through-hole joints

concepts.label: Arduino
concepts.definition: Open-source electronics platform combining easy-to-use hardware (MCU boards) and software (IDE)

concepts.label: Raspberry Pi
concepts.definition: Single-board computer with ARM processor running Linux for education and embedded projects

concepts.label: Breadboard
concepts.definition: Solderless prototyping board with spring-clip connections for temporary circuit construction

concepts.label: Perfboard
concepts.definition: Pre-drilled board with copper pads for permanent soldered prototypes

concepts.label: Stripboard
concepts.definition: Prototyping board with parallel copper strips for point-to-point soldered circuits (Veroboard)

concepts.label: Breakout Board
concepts.definition: Small PCB converting IC package (BGA, QFN) to breadboard-friendly pin headers

concepts.label: Shield
concepts.definition: Plug-in expansion board for Arduino or similar platform adding specific functionality

concepts.label: HAT
concepts.definition: Hardware Attached on Top: standardized expansion board for Raspberry Pi

concepts.label: Servo Motor
concepts.definition: Rotary actuator with position feedback for precise angular control in robotics

concepts.label: Stepper Motor
concepts.definition: Motor rotating in discrete steps for open-loop position control (e.g., NEMA 17)

concepts.label: DC Motor
concepts.definition: Motor converting DC electrical energy to rotational mechanical energy via commutation

concepts.label: Brushless DC Motor
concepts.definition: DC motor using electronic commutation instead of brushes for efficiency and longevity

concepts.label: Linear Actuator
concepts.definition: Motor converting rotational motion to linear push/pull motion via lead screw or other mechanism

concepts.label: Solenoid
concepts.definition: Electromechanical device using coil to produce linear push/pull motion of a plunger

concepts.label: Piezo Actuator
concepts.definition: Actuator using piezoelectric effect for nanometer-precision motion

concepts.label: Voltage Divider
concepts.definition: Two-resistor circuit producing output voltage as fraction of input voltage

concepts.label: Wheatstone Bridge
concepts.definition: Four-resistor bridge circuit for precise measurement of unknown resistance

concepts.label: RC Filter
concepts.definition: Resistor-capacitor circuit providing low-pass or high-pass frequency filtering

concepts.label: LC Filter
concepts.definition: Inductor-capacitor circuit for bandpass, bandstop, or higher-order filtering

concepts.label: Active Filter
concepts.definition: Filter circuit using op-amps for precise frequency response without inductors

concepts.label: EMI Filter
concepts.definition: Filter circuit suppressing electromagnetic interference at power input or signal boundaries

concepts.label: Snubber Circuit
concepts.definition: RC or RCD circuit suppressing voltage spikes from inductive switching

concepts.label: Clamping Circuit
concepts.definition: Circuit limiting signal voltage to a specified range using diodes

concepts.label: Rectifier Circuit
concepts.definition: Circuit converting AC to pulsating DC using diodes (half-wave, full-wave, bridge)

concepts.label: Bridge Rectifier
concepts.definition: Four-diode circuit converting AC to full-wave rectified DC

concepts.label: Voltage Multiplier
concepts.definition: Circuit using diodes and capacitors to produce output voltage higher than input (Cockcroft-Walton)

concepts.label: Crystal Filter
concepts.definition: Narrowband filter using quartz crystal resonators for radio receiver selectivity

concepts.label: SAW Filter
concepts.definition: Surface acoustic wave filter for precise RF bandpass filtering in mobile devices

concepts.label: BAW Filter
concepts.definition: Bulk acoustic wave filter (FBAR/SMR) for 5G and WiFi RF filtering

concepts.label: Balun
concepts.definition: Balanced-to-unbalanced transformer for converting between balanced and single-ended RF signals

concepts.label: Attenuator
concepts.definition: Passive network reducing signal amplitude by a specified amount in dB

concepts.label: Amplifier
concepts.definition: Circuit increasing signal amplitude using active devices (transistors, op-amps)

concepts.label: LNA
concepts.definition: Low-noise amplifier: first-stage RF amplifier minimizing added noise in receiver front-end

concepts.label: Power Amplifier
concepts.definition: Amplifier designed to deliver significant power to a load (speaker, antenna, motor)

concepts.label: Transimpedance Amplifier
concepts.definition: Amplifier converting input current to output voltage, used with photodiodes

concepts.label: Class D Amplifier
concepts.definition: Switching audio amplifier using PWM for >90% efficiency

concepts.label: Differential Amplifier
concepts.definition: Amplifier stage producing output proportional to the difference between two inputs

concepts.label: Programmable Gain Amplifier
concepts.definition: Amplifier with digitally selectable gain settings for dynamic range optimization

concepts.label: Audio Codec
concepts.definition: IC combining ADC and DAC for audio signal conversion (e.g., WM8960, PCM5102)

concepts.label: Video Codec IC
concepts.definition: IC encoding/decoding video streams (H.264, H.265) in hardware

concepts.label: RF Transceiver
concepts.definition: IC combining transmitter and receiver for wireless communication (e.g., CC1101, SX1276, nRF24L01)

concepts.label: Mixer
concepts.definition: RF circuit producing sum and difference frequencies from two input signals for frequency conversion

concepts.label: Synthesizer IC
concepts.definition: IC generating precise programmable frequencies using PLL and VCO for radio systems

concepts.label: PA Module
concepts.definition: Power amplifier module for boosting RF transmit signal to required output power

concepts.label: Duplexer
concepts.definition: RF filter allowing simultaneous transmit and receive on single antenna at different frequencies

concepts.label: Circulator
concepts.definition: Non-reciprocal RF device routing signal in one direction between three ports

concepts.label: RF Switch
concepts.definition: IC or device routing RF signals between paths under electronic control

concepts.label: Programmable Logic
concepts.definition: Category of ICs (PAL, GAL, CPLD, FPGA) whose logic function is configured post-manufacturing

concepts.label: LUT
concepts.definition: Look-up table: fundamental logic element in FPGAs implementing any boolean function of N inputs

concepts.label: CLB
concepts.definition: Configurable logic block: basic repeating logic unit in FPGA architecture containing LUTs and flip-flops

concepts.label: Block RAM
concepts.definition: Dedicated SRAM blocks in FPGA for on-chip data and FIFO storage

concepts.label: DSP Slice
concepts.definition: Dedicated multiply-accumulate block in FPGA for signal processing operations

concepts.label: SERDES
concepts.definition: Serializer/deserializer converting parallel data to high-speed serial and back in FPGAs and ASICs

concepts.label: Xilinx FPGA
concepts.definition: AMD/Xilinx FPGA families: Spartan, Artix, Kintex, Virtex, Zynq (with ARM cores)

concepts.label: Intel FPGA
concepts.definition: Intel/Altera FPGA families: Cyclone, Arria, Stratix, MAX (CPLD)

concepts.label: Lattice FPGA
concepts.definition: Lattice Semiconductor low-power FPGAs: iCE40, ECP5, CrossLink, Certus

concepts.label: Zynq SoC
concepts.definition: AMD/Xilinx programmable SoC combining ARM Cortex-A cores with FPGA fabric

concepts.label: VHDL
concepts.definition: VHSIC Hardware Description Language for describing digital circuit behavior and structure

concepts.label: Verilog
concepts.definition: Hardware description language for digital circuit design and FPGA/ASIC synthesis

concepts.label: SystemVerilog
concepts.definition: Extended Verilog with verification features for modern ASIC/FPGA design and verification

concepts.label: RTL
concepts.definition: Register-transfer level: abstraction describing digital circuit as data flow between registers

concepts.label: Synthesis
concepts.definition: Process converting HDL code to gate-level netlist for FPGA or ASIC implementation

concepts.label: Place and Route
concepts.definition: FPGA/ASIC tool step assigning logic to physical locations and creating wire connections

concepts.label: Bitstream
concepts.definition: Binary file programmed into FPGA to configure its logic and routing

concepts.label: Clock Domain Crossing
concepts.definition: Design challenge when signals cross between different clock domains in digital systems

concepts.label: Metastability
concepts.definition: Unstable state in flip-flop when setup/hold time is violated, requiring synchronization

concepts.label: FIFO
concepts.definition: First-in first-out buffer for rate matching and clock domain crossing in digital systems

concepts.label: DDS
concepts.definition: Direct digital synthesis generating precise waveforms from digital accumulator and DAC

concepts.label: Wireless Charging
concepts.definition: Power transfer without wires using inductive (Qi) or resonant magnetic coupling

concepts.label: Qi Charging
concepts.definition: Wireless Power Consortium inductive charging standard for smartphones and wearables

concepts.label: Inductive Coupling
concepts.definition: Energy transfer between coils via mutual inductance, basis for transformers and wireless charging

concepts.label: Impedance
concepts.definition: Complex opposition to AC current combining resistance and reactance (Z = R + jX)

concepts.label: Impedance Matching
concepts.definition: Designing source and load impedances for maximum power transfer or minimum reflection

concepts.label: Ohm's Law
